question: CPU scheduling is the basis of ___________
What are the two steps of a process execution?
An I/O bound program will typically have ____________

options.a: a few very short CPU bursts
options.d: a few very short I/O bursts

explanation: An I/O bound program frequently performs short computations (short CPU bursts) followed by waiting for I/O operations to complete.

question: A process is selected from the ______ queue by the ________ scheduler, to be executed.
In the following cases non – preemptive scheduling occurs?

options.a: When a process switches from the running state to the ready state
options.b: When a process goes from the running state to the waiting state
options.c: When a process switches from the waiting state to the ready state

question: The switching of the CPU from one process or thread to another is called ____________

options.a: the speed of dispatching a process from running to the ready state
options.b: the time of dispatching a process from running to ready state and keeping the CPU idle
options.c: the time to stop one process and start running another one

question: Scheduling is done so as to ____________
Scheduling is done so as to ____________

options.c: increase the duration of a specific amount of work

options.a: the total waiting time for a process to finish execution
options.b: the total time spent in the ready queue
options.c: the total time spent in the running queue
options.d: the total time from the completion till the submission of a process

question: Scheduling is done so as to ____________

options.d: there is no relation between scheduling and turnaround time

options.a: the total time in the blocked and waiting queues
options.b: the total time spent in the ready queue
options.c: the total time spent in the running queue
options.d: the total time from the completion till the submission of a process

question: Scheduling is done so as to ____________

options.b: keep the waiting time the same

options.a: the total time taken from the submission time till the completion time
options.b: the total time taken from the submission time till the first response is produced
options.c: the total time taken from submission time till the response is output
